data.title: MTQ Completes Disposal of PEMAC
data.description:
MTQ (M05) completed the disposal of PEMAC after the buyer paid a first tranche sale price of SG$3.2 million, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Monday.

In an earlier filing, the company had entered into a sale and purchase agreement with Sidi Investment Holdings for the disposal of its subsidiary, Pemac, for an aggregate consideration of SG$5.5 million.

Net proceeds from the disposal will be used to fund and reinforce primary operations, to reinvest in other core business segments and for general working capital purposes.

data.title: India bond yields to dip as RBI announces more liquidity infusion
data.description:
Indian government bond yields are expected to decline in early deals on Tuesday, as sentiment is likely to get a boost from the central bank's announcement of additional liquidity injection into the banking system.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield (IN067934G=CC) is likely to move between 6.40% and 6.44%, a trader at a private bank said, compared with previous close of 6.4445%.

India's financial markets were closed on Monday and will remain shut on Friday.

"The surprise announcement of an open market purchase of bonds for this week will support overall demand for debt," the trader said.

On Thursday, the Reserve Bank of India will buy bonds worth 400 billion rupees ($4.65 billion) and conduct a 43-day variable rate repo auction to boost liquidity.

The central bank already bought bonds worth 400 billion rupees in the first two weeks of April and is scheduled to buy a similar quantum in the next two weeks.

The RBI has infused over 5.41 trillion rupees into the banking system since the start of 2025 through debt purchases and foreign exchange swaps.

Last Wednesday, the RBI lowered its key repo rate for a second consecutive time, as expected, and changed its monetary policy stance to "accommodative" from "neutral", signalling room for more cuts ahead.

The central bank is expected to keep sufficient surplus in the banking system to ensure policy transmission and is looking at a level of around 1% of deposits.

Some economists expect a deeper-than-expected rate cut cycle on risks to growth and a benign inflation outlook.

India's retail inflation data is due later in the day.

RATES

India's overnight index swap rates are expected to decline further amid liquidity injection and bets of further monetary policy easing, traders said.

The one-year swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) eased 14 basis points last week to 5.78%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) declined two bps last week.

KEY INDICATORS:

** Brent crude futures were up 0.2% at $65.01 per barrel, after rising 0.2% in the previous session

** Ten-year U.S. Treasury yield at 4.3486%; two-year yield (US2YT=RR) at 3.8367%

** Indian states aim to raise 30 billion rupees through bond sale

** India to release March retail inflation data

** RBI to conduct 1-day variable rate repo auction for 250 billion rupees

($1 = 85.9510 Indian rupees)

data.title: Pro Medicus Unit Signs Multi-Year Research Collaboration Agreement for AI Accelerator Platform
data.description:
Pro Medicus said that its wholly-owned US unit, Visage Imaging, signed a multi-year research collaboration agreement with the University of California San Francisco as a framework for collaboration to facilitate the development and commercialization leveraging the Visage AI Accelerator platform, according to a Tuesday Australian bourse filing.

The agreement has the potential to develop artificial intelligence products to meet clinical goals and ultimately lead to better patient outcomes, Malte Westerhoff, Visage Imaging's global chief technology officer, said.

The research collaboration builds on the existing UCSF Visage 7 Viewer agreement.

Pro Medicus' shares fell past 1% in recent trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Indian benchmarks set to open higher after US tariff exemptions
data.description:
India's benchmark equity indexes are set to open higher on Tuesday after an extended weekend as U.S. President Donald Trump has exempted electronics from steep "reciprocal" tariffs.

Gift Nifty futures (GIFc1) were trading at 23,299.5 as of 8:03 a.m. IST, indicating an about 2% rise from the Nifty 50's Friday close of 22,828.55. Indian markets were shut on Monday for a local holiday.

Global equities have been on a volatile path as Trump's tariff flip-flops have triggered uncertainty over their impact on global growth and inflation.

Over the weekend, the U.S. excluded smartphones, computers and other electronic items from "reciprocal" tariffs, sparking a relief rally in global equities.

Trump had earlier this month announced steep tariffs on all U.S. imports, but later paused them for 90 days on several countries excluding China.

Meanwhile, the U.S. is setting the stage to proceed with tariffs on semiconductors and medicines under the national security umbrella.

The uncertain global trade and tariff policies have led Morgan Stanley to lower India's fiscal year 2026 GDP growth projection by 40 basis points to 6.1% and cut the December 2025 Sensex target by 12% to 82,000.

Auto and pharma stocks will be in focus on the day amid tariff-related developments.

Trump has suggested he might grant exemptions on the 25% levies for automobiles that are already in place.

Tata Motors and some auto part makers like Samvardhana Motherson International , Sona BLW Precision Forgings and Sansera Engineering were seen as taking a hit from Trump's auto tariffs.

STOCKS TO WATCH

** Bank stocks will be in focus as HDFC Bank , State Bank of India and others lowered deposit rates, which Jefferies said is key to easing risks regarding a drop in net interest margin.

** Zydus Lifesciences expands into France, establishes a new unit via acquisition of an orthopedic firm

** Ashoka Buildcon has been declared the lowest bidder for a project worth 5.69 billion rupees ($66 million) by Central Railway

($1 = 85.9570 Indian rupees)

data.title: South Korean Won Weakens on Dollar Rebound
data.description:
The South Korean won weakened to around 1,424 per dollar on Tuesday, retreating from over four-month highs reached in the previous session, weighed down by a rebound in the greenback.

The US dollar regained strength following President Trump’s decision to exempt key technology products from reciprocal tariffs, along with reports suggesting a potential pause in his proposed 25% auto import levies.

Meanwhile, in South Korea, the government announced significant support measures in response to growing concerns over US policy uncertainties and intensifying global competition.

The package includes KRW 33 trillion for the semiconductor industry from 26 trillion won announced in 2024, along with financial assistance for chipmakers totaling KRW 20 trillion, compared to KRW 17 trillion.

Finance Minister Choi Sang-mok emphasized that Seoul will 'actively consult with the US' on its Section 232 probes into chip and biopharmaceutical imports to minimize the impact on South Korea’s key industries.

data.title: Japan futures tick lower on yen strength, potential US tariff exemptions limit losses
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures edged lower on Tuesday, pressured by a stronger yen and a firmer supply outlook, though possible relief for U.S. automobile-related levies cushioned the fall for the tyre-making material.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) September rubber contract , ended morning trade 2.3 yen lower, or 0.76%, at 300.8 yen ($2.10) per kg.

  • The May rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) eased 115 yuan, or 0.76%, to 14,950 yuan ($2,044.36) per metric ton.

  • The most active May butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) fell 165 yuan, or 1.41%, to 11,560 yuan ($1,580.79) per ton.

  • Geopolitical tensions and recessionary concerns continue to weigh on markets, a Singapore-based trader said.

  • The dollar was last at 143.53 yen , remaining close to the six-month low of 142.05 it touched on Friday as investors struggled to make sense of the back-and-forth changes on U.S. tariffs.

  • A stronger currency makes yen-denominated assets less affordable to overseas buyers.

  • "With seasonal increases in raw material supply approaching, rubber prices are unlikely to rise significantly in the near term," the trader added.

  • Production areas in Yunnan have entered harvesting, while overseas production areas will begin trial harvesting soon, said Chinese rubber sales portal Natural Rubber Network.

  • Rubber crops usually undergo a season of low production from February to May, before a peak harvesting period that lasts until September.

  • Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump said he was considering a modification to the 25% tariffs imposed on foreign automobile and auto parts imports from Mexico, Canada, and other places.

  • Automobile sales could influence the intensity of automobile manufacturing, which involves using rubber tyres.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for May delivery last traded at 168.7 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.6%.

($1 = 143.1700 yen)

($1 = 7.3128 Chinese yuan)

data.title: J. Front Retailing Profit Jumps 39% in Fiscal 2025 on Better Sales and Gain on Step Acquisition
data.description:
J. Front Retailing's profit attributable to owners of the parent jumped nearly 39% to 41.4 billion yen for the fiscal year ended Feb. 28, from 29.9 billion yen a year ago driven by department store and shopping center businesses, and a gain on step acquisition.

The departmental store operator's diluted earnings per share (EPS) rose to 160.15 yen from 114.06 yen a year ago, according to a filing on Monday.

Sales revenue jumped 8.6% to 441.9 billion yen from 407.0 billion yen a year earlier. Gross sales gained 10.1% to 1.268 trillion yen.

For the six months ended Aug. 31, it forecasts profit to decline 36% to 18.5 billion yen or 73.79 yen per share while sales revenue to gain 7.2% to 224.4 billion yen. For the fiscal year ending Feb. 28, 2026, the company expects attributable profit to decline 27.6% to 30.0 billion yen or 120.93 yen per share, while sales revenue is expected to rise 3.9% to 459.0 billion yen.

The company expects to pay interim and year-end dividends of 27 yen per share, each, for the current fiscal year.

It declared a final dividend of 30 yen per share for fiscal 2025, payable from May 8.

data.title: Trust Chem Opens 298% Higher on Shenzhen Debut
data.description:
Trust Chem opened 298% higher on its first trading day on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange Tuesday.

The pigment and acid dye manufacturer priced its initial public offering of 23,434,890 shares at 12.80 yuan apiece.

data.title: Australian Dollar Extends Rally on Risk-On Mood
data.description:
The Australian dollar climbed toward $0.635 on Tuesday, marking its fifth consecutive session of gains as improved global risk appetite fueled a rally in antipodean currencies.

The upbeat sentiment followed US President Donald Trump’s recent decision to exempt key technology products from his reciprocal tariffs, alongside reports that his administration may pause the planned 25% tariffs on auto imports.

However, uncertainty lingered after the US Commerce Department launched a national security investigation into semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports.

On the domestic front, minutes from the Reserve Bank of Australia's April meeting offered little clarity on the timing of the next interest rate move.

Policymakers pointed to elevated levels of uncertainty both at home and abroad, keeping future policy decisions data-dependent.

data.title: Australia's Evolution Mining hits record high on extension for gold operations
data.description:
** Shares of Evolution Mining rise as much as 2.4% to record high of A$8.09; last up 1.9%

** Stock is among the top gainers on benchmark ASX 200 , which is up 0.5%

** Gold miner says its top gold producing Cowal operations receive approval to extend functioning to 2042

** Total capital investment approved is A$430 million ($272.62 million) over the next 7 years with FY25 capital guidance for project updated to A$65-A$70 million

** Separately, co reports quarterly total gold production of 179,778 Oz vs. 185,252 Oz last year

** Stock up 67.2% YTD

($1 = 1.5773 Australian dollars)

data.title: Shandong Gold Mining rises on robust earnings estimates
data.description:
** Hong Kong shares of Shandong Gold Mining rise 4% to HK$23.2, their highest since August 2020, and on course for sixth consecutive session of gains

** Shanghai-listed stock climbs 0.4% to 31.01 yuan

** The Chinese gold miner expects its Q1 net profit up 35.7%-61.5% y/y

** Hang Seng Commerce & Industry Index and Hang Seng Index both gain 0.1%

** YTD, Hong Kong stock up 83.9%, Shanghai shares up 35.8%

data.title: Market Chatter: Small Businesses Sue to Block Trump's Tariffs
data.description:
Five US small businesses-from a New York wine importer to a Virginia educational kit maker-are suing in the US Court of International Trade to stop US President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs, Reuters reported Tuesday.

The group argues Trump overstepped constitutional limits with his "Liberation Day" tariffs and duties separately levied on China, claiming that only Congress can impose taxes like tariffs, according to the report.

"No one person should have the power to impose taxes that have such vast global economic consequences," Reuters cited Jeffrey Schwab of Liberty Justice Center, which filed the lawsuit on behalf of the group.

A similar lawsuit was filed in the Florida federal court, where a small business owner sought to block tariffs imposed on China, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Eyes Bullish Continuation—Is $90K Within Reach?
data.description:
Bitcoin price started a fresh increase above the $83,500 zone. BTC is now consolidating gains and might attempt to clear the $85,500 resistance.

  • Bitcoin started a fresh increase above the $83,500 zone.
  • The price is trading above $83,000 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average.
  • There is a connecting bullish trend line forming with support at $84,200 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair (data feed from Kraken).
  • The pair could start another increase if it clears the $85,500 zone.

Bitcoin Price Eyes More Gains

Bitcoin price started a fresh increase above the $82,500 zone. BTC formed a base and gained pace for a move above the $83,000 and $83,500 resistance levels.

The bulls pumped the price above the $84,500 resistance. A high was formed at $85,850 and the price recently started a downside correction. There was a move below the $84,000 support. The price dipped below the 23.6% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $78,600 swing low to the $85,850 high.

However, the bulls were active near the $83,000 zone and the price recovered losses. Bitcoin price is now trading above $83,500 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average. There is also a connecting bullish trend line forming with support at $84,200 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair.

On the upside, immediate resistance is near the $85,000 level. The first key resistance is near the $85,500 level. The next key resistance could be $86,200. A close above the $86,200 resistance might send the price further higher. In the stated case, the price could rise and test the $87,500 resistance level. Any more gains might send the price toward the $88,000 level.

Another Rejection In BTC?

If Bitcoin fails to rise above the $85,500 resistance zone, it could start another decline. Immediate support on the downside is near the $84,200 level and the trend line. The first major support is near the $83,200 level.

The next support is now near the $82,200 zone and the 50% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $78,600 swing low to the $85,850 high. Any more losses might send the price toward the $81,500 support in the near term. The main support sits at $80,800.

Technical indicators:

Hourly MACD – The MACD is now gaining pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BTC/USD is now above the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $84,200, followed by $83,500.

Major Resistance Levels – $85,500 and $85,850.

data.title: Singapore Dollar Weakens Slightly as Traders Weigh Mixed U.S. Tariff SignalsMarket Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar weakens slightly against its U.S. counterpart as traders weigh mixed signals on U.S. tariffs. "After announcing a temporary reprieve on the electronics tariffs, President Trump said he is exploring possible temporary exemptions to his tariffs on imported cars and parts to give car makers more time to set up U.S. factories," CBA's Carol Kong says in a research report. However, "tariffs on imports of semiconductors and pharmaceuticals are still in the pipeline," the economist and currency strategist says. Uncertainty over U.S. policy-making and the global economic outlook is still high, Kong adds. USD/SGD is 0.1% higher at 1.3161. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0236 GMT - The Singapore dollar weakens slightly against its U.S. counterpart as traders weigh mixed signals on U.S. tariffs. "After announcing a temporary reprieve on the electronics tariffs, President Trump said he is exploring possible temporary exemptions to his tariffs on imported cars and parts to give car makers more time to set up U.S. factories," CBA's Carol Kong says in a research report. However, "tariffs on imports of semiconductors and pharmaceuticals are still in the pipeline," the economist and currency strategist says. Uncertainty over U.S. policy-making and the global economic outlook is still high, Kong adds. USD/SGD is 0.1% higher at 1.3161. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0234 GMT - The ongoing U.S. tariff uncertainties could weigh on Malaysia's economy, particularly through supply chain disruptions and weakened external demand, Public IB analyst Eltricia Foong says in a note. While most of Malaysia's exports to the U.S., comprising largely electronics and machinery, are exempt for now, uncertainty over the duration of these exemptions may dampen investor confidence, she reckons. Foong lowers Malaysia's 2025 GDP forecast to 4.2% from 4.9%, citing trade risks and market volatility. Public IB maintains its KLCI end-2025 target at 1630, favoring stocks like CIMB Group, Telekom Malaysia, Tenaga Nasional and Gamuda. The KLCI is 0.1% higher at 1482.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0212 GMT - The euro may have kick-started a multimonth uptrend against the dollar, based on charts, Oanda's Kelvin Wong says in a note. The currency pair staged a strong rally on Friday, marking a daily and weekly close above the upper boundary of a long-term descending channel, the senior market analyst says. Also, its recent sharp rebound happened after failing to drop below the 200-day moving average on March 27, signaling a major bullish breakout, the analyst says. Medium-term resistance levels are pegged in the 1.1715-1.1755 area and at the 1.1910 level, the analyst adds. EUR/USD is 0.2% lower at 1.1324. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0152 GMT - Chinese President Xi Jinping's state visit to Malaysia could strengthen bilateral trade ties, especially as both nations may look to diversify away from the U.S., MIDF Research says in a note. The visit comes at a critical time amid rising trade tariffs and global economic tensions, the research house says. Xi's visit may boost Belt and Road initiatives in Malaysia and enhance connectivity and regional integration, it reckons. Malaysia's strong semiconductor and electronics sectors may continue to attract Chinese investment, driven by ongoing supply-chain diversification, it adds. MIDF notes that sectors likely to benefit include construction, technology and property, while tourism-related companies such as Capital A may also gain from stronger bilateral relations. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0118 GMT - There are murmurs that the Japanese government is considering giving cash handouts to protect households from the impact of higher U.S. tariffs on prices and the economy. However, such a measure may not be effective in boosting spending as the country's consumers are still very cautious, having faced long-term economic stagnation in the not so distant past. Japanese households used only about 20% of the 100,000 yen handout provided during the pandemic for shopping and saved the rest, according to estimates by the Cabinet Office. Opposition parties say any one-time cash payment would just be a gimmick to draw voters' attention before the Upper House election.(megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0044 GMT - The yen weakens against most other G-10 and Asian currencies in early trade amid improving risk appetite spurred by overnight comments from U.S. and Fed officials. President Trump indicated that he's looking at offering some relief to auto sector by scaling back fresh tariffs, NAB's Ray Attrill says in commentary. Also, Fed governor Waller made dovish remarks about prioritizing the hit to growth over inflation if Trump proceeds with his April 2 tariff announcements, the head of FX Research adds. USD/JPY rises 0.3% to 143.45 and AUD/JPY adds 0.2% to 90.74, according to FactSet. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0026 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi rises 0.4% to 2466.31 in early trade, as auto and electronics stocks advance. Retail investors are net buyers. Sentiment is partly supported by the government's plan to draw up a bigger-than-expected extra budget to support the economy and for disaster relief following the wildfires in March. Index heavyweight Samsung Electronics rises 1.1%. Carmakers Hyundai Motor and Kia add 2.5% and 2.9%, respectively. Home-appliance giant LG Electronics gains 2.5%. USD/KRW is 0.1% higher at 1424.90 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is down 0.5 basis point at 2.691%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0013 GMT - JGBs consolidate in early Tokyo trade, with focus on U.S.-China trade conflict and today's auction of Y1 trillion in 20-year sovereign debt. The hardline stances of the U.S. and China are unlikely to suddenly soften, which means major stress for financial markets, SMBC Nikko Securities' Ataru Okumura says in a recent research report. Also, it's unclear whether the 20-year JGB auction will attract investors, the senior Japan rates strategist says. "We advise keeping a lookout for increased volatility around the time of the auction," Okumura adds. The five-year JGB yield is unchanged at 0.810%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2342 GMT - Japanese stocks may rise as fears about an escalating trade conflict between the U.S. and China ease. Nikkei futures are up 0.7% at 34410 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 143.12, compared with 142.54 as of Monday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on U.S. tariff news ahead of the Japanese earnings season set to start next week. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 1.2% to 33982.36 on Monday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

2222 GMT - Economists at Pantheon Macro fall close to the Wall Street consensus with expectations of a 1% rise in U.S. retail sales in March, in data expected for release Wednesday morning. But stripping out categories can help find the underlying trend. Exclude car sales, and the expectation falls to a minus 0.1% contraction. Yet that measure itself is strongly influenced by a big fall in gas prices last month, which pulls down spending at the pump. It can be helpful to look at the retail "control group," which excludes cars, gasoline, food services and building materials. Pantheon sees this measure rising 0.6% in March, down from a 1% increase in February. They expect weaker readings ahead in 2Q. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

2104 GMT - A new Canada political poll, issued two weeks prior to the country's April 28 national election, indicates voters' concern over President Trump's trade policy is easing - to the detriment of the incumbent Liberals, which have held a solid lead for most of the campaign. Angus Reid Institute says the Liberals enjoy 45% support versus 39% for the Conservatives, marking a narrower lead. Meanwhile, the poll suggests that 29% of voters identify U.S. relations and Trump trade policy as a top issue, down from a peak of 41% in mid-March. Meanwhile, cost of living and health care are the top two electoral issues, at 56% and 38%. "This race may be shifting slightly," Angus Reid says. (paul.vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

1951 GMT - Treasury yields retreat as global markets take a breather after Friday's partial tariff relief and data today showing stable long-term inflation expectations. The Fed's monthly survey on consumers' inflation expectations showed a small increase in the year-ahead forecast in March, but no change in the longer run estimates, easing fears that the central bank could be reluctant to cut interest rates to support the economy. No major data point is due tomorrow. March retail sales and industrial production are due Wednesday. The 10-year yield falls 0.131 percentage point to 4.361% and the two-year declines 0.119 p.p. to 3.830%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Aeon Co to Make AEON Mall a Wholly Owned Subsidiary via Share Exchange
data.description:
Aeon Co will acquire all remaining shares of AEON Mall through a share exchange, making it a wholly owned subsidiary effective July 1, according to a bourse filing on Monday.

AEON Mall shareholders will receive 0.65 AEON shares for each AEON Mall share, with AEON Mall shares delisting on June 27.

The move aims to enhance corporate value by leveraging AEON Group resources, expanding business opportunities, and unifying digital marketing and real estate strategies.

Independent third-party valuations and a special committee ensured fairness, with negotiations raising the exchange ratio from an initial 0.57 to 0.65, read the filing.

data.title: Leader Environmental Technologies Reports Three Consecutive Years of Losses; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Leader Environmental Technologies reported three consecutive years of losses, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Monday.

The company's average daily market capitalization was at SG$66.4 million, avoiding being placed on the SGX Watch-List.

Companies with an average daily market capitalization of less than SG$40 million along with three consecutive years of losses are placed on the watch-list.

Shares of the company were down nearly 3% in morning trade Tuesday.

data.title: NZ Dollar Hits Over 4-Month High
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar rose to around $0.590 on Tuesday, gaining for the sixth straight session and reaching its highest level since early December 2024, as risk sentiment continued to improve.

This followed the US announcement of a tariff rollback on consumer electronics, along with Trump’s suggestion that he may grant exemptions on existing auto-related tariffs.

However, caution lingered as the US Commerce Department launched national security probes into semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports.

Domestically, investors are awaiting key economic data this week—including the Q1 consumer inflation report and March trade balance—for further insight into price pressures and overall economic conditions.

This follows growing signs of a slowing economy, with the services sector contracting for a second straight month and card spending declining, after the RBNZ’s recent rate cut to 3.5%.

data.title: Japanese Yen Weakens as Risk Sentiment Improves
data.description:
The Japanese yen slipped past 143 per dollar on Tuesday, retreating from recent highs as a rebound in risk appetite dampened demand for the safe-haven currency.

The shift in sentiment followed US President Donald Trump’s decision to exempt key technology products from newly imposed reciprocal tariffs, with reports also suggesting a potential pause on his 25% auto import levies.

Despite the improved tone, markets remained cautious after the US Commerce Department launched a national security investigation into semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports, industries deeply intertwined with Asian supply chains.

Investors are also closely monitoring upcoming trade negotiations between Washington and Tokyo.

Japan’s chief trade negotiator, Akazawa Ryosei, is set to meet with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer later this week.

Japan, which currently faces a reduced 10% tariff rate, is expected to push for more favorable terms amid ongoing talks.

data.title: South Korea's ICT Exports Rise 9.4% in March
data.description:
South Korea's exports of information and communications technology products rose 9.4% to $20.6 billion in March from a year earlier, the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy said Monday.

Imports of ICT goods increased 6.8% to $12.2 billion, which led to a trade surplus of $8.4 billion in the month, the release said.

Key tech exports such as semiconductors, displays, mobile phones, and computers witnessed growth, while communication devices slightly declined. The semiconductor rebound was driven by low inventory and rising demand for advanced chips, while displays recovered on new phone launches. Mobile phone and computer parts shipments rose on global demand, but communication device exports slipped due to reduced parts supply.

data.title: May Meeting Seen as 'Opportune Time' to Revisit Monetary Policy, RBA Says in April Minutes
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) said its meeting in May would be an "opportune time to revisit the monetary policy setting" as it monitors the risks from the rapidly evolving trade developments and their impact on domestic conditions, minutes from its April meeting showed on Tuesday.

In a widely expected move, the RBA maintained the official cash rate at 4.1% on April 1. That was before the Trump Administration's "Liberation Day" tariffs, which was later rescinded.

"In addition, members judged that it was not appropriate at this stage for monetary policy to react to the potential risks that could move outcomes in either direction," the RBA said.

The bank's board members noted that while it was not possible to determine the timing of the next move, future decisions would be based on new information and how it would impact the economic outlook.

"Given this, members agreed that it would be helpful if the Board's public communication following the meeting made it clear that the outcome of its next decision was not predetermined," the RBA added.

Analysts are projecting more rate cuts from the RBA in the wake of the US tariffs.

In an April 10 report, NAB Economics said the RBA is forecast to deliver a 50-basis-point cut in May, followed by 25 basis points at each meeting in July, August, November, and 2026, lowering the official cash rate to 2.6% by February 2026 from the current 4.1%.

data.title: India's GSPC seeks LNG cargo for May delivery, sources say
data.description:
India's Gujarat State Petroleum Corp (GSPC) is seeking a liquefied natural gas (LNG) cargo for delivery in May, said two industry sources on Tuesday.

The Indian state-owned firm is seeking the cargo for delivery in the second half of May, said one of the sources.

The tender closes on April 15.

data.title: Casino firm Star Entertainment posts bigger-than-expected first-half loss
data.description:
Australia's Star Entertainment reported a bigger-than-expected first-half loss on Friday, as regulatory restrictions continue to affect the firm, particularly with the introduction of mandatory carded play and cash limits at its casinos.

The embattled casino operator posted an underlying loss of A$136 million ($86.18 million) for the six months ended December 31, compared with the Visible Alpha consensus of a loss of A$93.9 million.

Star Entertainment had reported an underlying profit of A$25 million in the same period a year earlier.

($1 = 1.5780 Australian dollars)

data.title: Star Entertainment: Shareholder Meeting Regarding Bally's Investment Targeted for Late June
Star Entertainment: Pursuing Range of Initiatives to Improve Performance and Enhance Liquidity Position
De.mem Posts March Quarter Cash Receipts of AU$7.9 Million, Shares Up 9%
data.description:
De.mem reported March quarter cash receipts of about AU$7.9 million, up 20% from last year, according to a Tuesday Australian bourse filing.

The company reported an increasing cash balance of about AU$3.7 million as of March 31, up from AU$3.6 million on Dec. 31 2024.

The company's shares rose 9% in recent Tuesday trade.

data.title: CTF Life Supports 10 Local Schools with Donations of Dr MAC Classroom Reading Programme and Learning Living Chinese Programme
data.description:
HONG KONG, Apr 15, 2025 - (ACN Newswire) - CTF Life participated in the donation ceremony for the Dr MAC Classroom Reading Programme and Learning Living Chinese Programme jointly hosted by Jing Yuan Charity Foundation and Parenting Headline at Buddhist Chi King Primary School in Kowloon Bay. As a key focus of CTF Life's Seeds for Future 2025 programme, this initiative provides illustrated books and Chinese language learning materials to 10 local schools to promote Chinese culture, enhance students' language proficiency, instil positive values and nurture life education through reading - all to foster a harmonious and inclusive community.

The Dr MAC Classroom Reading Programme and Learning Living Chinese Programme are flagship charity projects initiated by the Jing Yuan Charity Foundation, representing the rich legacy of Dr. Cheng Yu-Tung, legendary leader of Chow Tai Fook Group. The Chinese language materials provided under the Learning Living Chinese Programme were developed by a professional team of over 50 members led by renowned Chinese tutor Mr. Jayden Lam Yat Yan. The content integrates Chinese historical and cultural knowledge while aligning with the local primary and secondary school curriculum. Presented in an engaging, step-by-step structure with detailed illustrations, the materials also come with audio pens that explain historical anecdotes in a reader-friendly way, supporting school-based Chinese teaching and self-directed learning.

Man Kit Ip, Executive Director and Chief Executive Officer of CTF Life, said: 'CTF Life has long adhered to Dr. Cheng Yu-Tung's spirit of 'Diligence, Integrity, and Dedication' and actively fulfils its social responsibilities. Through this donation of educational books and materials to local primary schools, we aim to use reading as a bridge to impart positive values and life education to the next generation, promote Chinese culture and language, and strengthen local students' cultural confidence and sense of identity, thereby creating greater shared value for society.'

Peter Cheng Kar Shing, Chairman of Chow Tai Fook Charity Foundation and Project Management Committee Chairman of Jing Yuan Charity Foundation, said: 'The Dr MAC Classroom Reading Programme and Learning Living Chinese Programme are designed to advocate positive life education while enhancing students' interest in learning Chinese. We hope these initiatives will pass on Dr. Cheng Yu-Tung's spirit of 'Diligence, Integrity, and Dedication' to children and cultivate positive values in their lives. Since the commencement of the Programmes in 2022, we have already benefited over 30,000 students from nearly 60 primary schools and kindergartens. We encourage students to make good use of these resources to broaden their knowledge and gain meaningful insights.'

A total of 300 sets (12 books per set) of Dr MAC Classroom illustrated books with audio pens and programme bookshelves were donated to 10 schools in CTF Life's name. The beneficiary schools are Buddhist Chi King Primary School, Buddhist Wong Cheuk Um Primary School, Buddhist Lam Bing Yim Memorial School, Buddhist Lim Kim Tian Memorial Primary School, Buddhist Wing Yan School, Buddhist Chan Wing Kan Memorial School, Shun Tak Fraternal Association Wu Siu Kui Memorial Primary School, Shun Tak Fraternal Association Lee Kam Primary School, Shun Tak Fraternal Association Wu Mien Tuen Primary School and Po Kok Primary School.

Previously, CTF Life collaborated with Jing Yuan Charity Foundation and Chow Tai Fook Charity Foundation to support the publication of the 'Cheng Yu-Tung: A Lifetime of Diligence, Integrity and Dedication' to promote his life philosophy.

About CTF Life

Chow Tai Fook Life Insurance Company Limited ('CTF Life') is proud of its rich, 40-year legacy in Hong Kong. CTF Life is a wholly-owned subsidiary of CTF Services Limited ('CTFS') (Hong Kong Stock Code: 659) and one of the most well-established life insurance companies in Hong Kong. As a member of Chow Tai Fook Enterprises Limited, CTF Life consistently strengthens its collaboration with the diverse conglomerate of the Cheng family ('Chow Tai Fook Group' or 'the Group') to support customers and their loved ones in navigating life's journey with personalised planning solutions, lifelong protection and diverse lifestyle experiences. By leveraging the Group's robust financial strength and strategic investments across the globe, CTF Life aspires to become a leading insurance company in Asia while continuously creating value beyond insurance.

data.title: Star Entertainment: Targeting Completion of Transaction to Exit Destination Brisbane Consortium by End of June
Malaysia's Economic Growth Seen Weighed by U.S. Tariff RisksMarket Talk
data.description: The ongoing U.S. tariff uncertainties could weigh on Malaysia's economy, particularly through supply chain disruptions and weakened external demand, Public IB analyst Eltricia Foong says in a note. While most of Malaysia's exports to the U.S., comprising largely electronics and machinery, are exempt for now, uncertainty over the duration of these exemptions may dampen investor confidence, she reckons. Foong lowers Malaysia's 2025 GDP forecast to 4.2% from 4.9%, citing trade risks and market volatility. Public IB maintains its KLCI end-2025 target at 1630, favoring stocks like CIMB Group, Telekom Malaysia, Tenaga Nasional and Gamuda. The KLCI is 0.1% higher at 1482.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)
